name: Bologna Bombing 1980
description: The Bologna bombing was a terrorist attack that occurred on August 2, 1980, at the Bologna Centrale railway station in Bologna, Italy, killing 85 people and injuring over 200. It remains one of the deadliest terrorist acts in Italian history. Officially, the bombing was attributed to the neo-fascist terrorist group Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari (NAR). However, various alternative theories and conspiracy claims persist, questioning the official narrative. Proponents of these theories suggest involvement of other actors, including elements within Italian intelligence agencies (SISMI and SISDE), the P2 Masonic lodge, and even foreign entities. Evidence cited by conspiracy theorists includes inconsistencies in the investigation, alleged manipulation of evidence, the relatively light sentences initially given to some accused, and claims of suppressed witness testimonies. These theories often paint a picture of a 'strategy of tension' (strategia della tensione), where covert actions were orchestrated to destabilize the country and justify authoritarian measures. Mainstream scholarship and legal judgments largely uphold the NAR's responsibility, though acknowledging potential connections to broader networks and the possible involvement of individuals who were never brought to justice. The debate continues to be politically charged and emotionally sensitive in Italy.
subtitle: Italy's deadliest post-war terrorist attack.

claims.claim: The official narrative blaming NAR is incomplete or false.
claims.evidence: Inconsistencies in the investigation, manipulation of evidence, light sentences initially given to some accused, suppressed witness testimonies.
claims.criticism: Legal proceedings have largely confirmed NAR's involvement, although questions about accomplices remain. Inconsistencies are often explained as errors or limitations of the investigation.

claims.claim: Italian intelligence agencies (SISMI, SISDE) were involved in the planning or execution.
claims.evidence: Alleged connections between NAR members and intelligence agents, claims of a 'strategy of tension' aimed at destabilizing Italy.
claims.criticism: While some connections between NAR and rogue elements within intelligence agencies have been documented, direct involvement in the bombing remains unproven. The 'strategy of tension' is a complex and contested historical interpretation.

claims.claim: The P2 Masonic lodge played a role in the bombing.
claims.evidence: Connections between some NAR members and P2, the lodge's history of involvement in Italian political scandals.
claims.criticism: Although some connections exist, a direct causal link between P2 and the bombing has not been definitively established. The lodge's influence on Italian politics is undeniable, but its precise role in specific events is often speculative.

keyFigures.name: Giusva Fioravanti
keyFigures.role: Leader of NAR, convicted of the bombing.

keyFigures.name: Francesca Mambro
keyFigures.role: NAR member, convicted of the bombing.

keyFigures.name: Licio Gelli
keyFigures.role: Grand Master of P2 Masonic lodge, suspected of involvement.

keyFigures.name: Giovanni Pellegrino
keyFigures.role: President of the Parliamentary Commission on Terrorism, investigated the bombing.

timeline.event: Discovery of Propaganda Due (P2) Masonic lodge.
First convictions of NAR members for the bombing.
Further investigations and debates continue regarding the bombing's perpetrators and motives.

sources.author: Philip Willan
sources.work: Puppetmasters: The Political Use of Terrorism in Italy
sources.title: Philip Willan

sources.author: Franco Ferraresi
sources.work: Threats to Democracy: The Radical Right in Italy After the War
sources.title: Franco Ferraresi
